title: Avoid Source Confusion: - Keep a Log: Note Where You Heard or Read Key
subtitle: Track Information Sources
description:
To avoid source confusion:
- Keep a log: Note where you heard or read key ideas.
- Cross-reference: Before sharing information, double-check its accuracy and origin.
- Ask yourself: “Am I mixing this up with another memory or story?”

Example: Before presenting a new concept, confirm whether it’s from your research or a colleague’s suggestion.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
faq.answerHtml: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Create a Source Log
howTo.summary: Open the Brali LifeOS app and create a folder named 'Source Log'. This will be your dedicated space for tracking information sources.

howTo.title: Log Key Ideas
howTo.summary: Whenever you encounter a key idea, log it immediately using a single line format: date, short label, context, origin tag, and link. This helps preserve accurate attribution.

howTo.title: Review Your Logs
howTo.summary: At the end of each day, review your logged items to ensure clarity and completeness. This will help reinforce the habit and improve your source tracking.

howTo.title: Cross-Check Before Sharing
howTo.summary: Before sharing any information, cross-check your logs to verify the source. This reduces the risk of misattribution and enhances your credibility.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/track-information-sources/ to keep track of your progress.
